full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Can you draft starter Python code for a simplified LASVM-I style learner: apply an RBF feature map, compute z = y * (w dot phi(x)), skip updates when z > 1 or z < s, and update only in the active interval with light regularization? Also note which paper step each code block is approximating.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me with starter code for the filtered online update loop, which I modified, tested across seeds, and annotated against the paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Compared the final code path against Section 6 of the paper and reran the notebook until the outputs were consistent.

top_5_prompts.why_important: This prompt makes it clear that code assistance was used, but only as a starting point for an implementation I still had to adapt and verify.
